generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, constants) visible in the AADL model or defined within the current AGREE annex.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) have correct types (int, real, bool) matching their expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` to define helper expressions and `const` to define named constants.
Range checks typically use boolean expressions: `lower_limit <= value <= upper_limit`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL elements (ports, features, etc.) as part of a repair.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within component implementations.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by modifying the AADL model.
